title: Compliance Posture Score Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.36 (compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security) obliges organisations to regularly review policy compliance. This UC trends portfolio-wide posture scores and provides the longitudinal signal management needs to evidence that review.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.5.36 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.1: Compliance Posture Score Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.1' running on index compliance and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: GV.OV-03 requires that cybersecurity risk management performance is measured, evaluated, and reviewed by leadership. Quarterly posture trending across frameworks is exactly the evidence this subcategory calls for.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF GV.OV-03 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.1: Compliance Posture Score Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.1' running on index compliance and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), custom HTTP poller, or indexed CSV from Archer/ServiceNow GRC
spl:
index=compliance OR index=grc sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture") earliest=-730d
| eval fw=case(
    like(framework,"%NIST%"),"NIST_CSF",
    like(framework,"%ISO%"),"ISO27001",
    like(framework,"%SOC%"),"SOC2",
    1=1,framework)
| where isnum(overall_score)
| bin _time span=90d
| stats avg(overall_score) as avg_score by _time, fw
| stats avg(avg_score) as portfolio_score by _time
| timechart span=90d avg(portfolio_score) as avg_portfolio_score
| trendline sma3(avg_portfolio_score) as sma_posture
| predict avg_portfolio_score as posture_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=4
```
```spl
index=compliance OR index=grc sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture") earliest=-730d
| eval fw=case(like(framework,"%NIST%"),"NIST_CSF",like(framework,"%ISO%"),"ISO27001",like(framework,"%SOC%"),"SOC2",1=1,framework)
| timechart span=3mon avg(overall_score) as avg_score by fw
| trendline sma2(NIST_CSF) as roll_NIST_CSF sma2(ISO27001) as roll_ISO27001 sma2(SOC2) as roll_SOC2
description: Rolling quarterly posture scores across NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and SOC 2 show whether investments in controls are improving measurable outcomes—not just checkbox activity—so executives and regulators see trajectory, not a one-time snapshot.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001, NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance, grc and sourcetypes (, compliance:framework_score, grc:posture. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Land GRC or continuous-control scores into `compliance` or `grc` with stable `framework` labels and numeric `overall_score` (0–100); (2) align calendar quarters to your audit cycle (`span=90d` vs fiscal); (3) schedule weekly and snapshot results to a summary index for year-over-year evidence; (4) validate `predict` against low-volume series—disable or widen `period` if confidence bands explode; (5) pair the portfolio panel with the by-framework panel for board-ready trending. Indexes required: `index=compliance`, `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=(`, `sourcetype=compliance:framework_score`, `sourcetype=grc:posture`.
visualization: Line or area chart (portfolio score, `sma_posture`, `posture_forecast`), multiseries line (scores by `fw`), overlay confidence bands from `predict`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), custom HTTP poller, or indexed CSV from Archer/ServiceNow GRC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` OR `index=grc` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture")` — fields `framework`, `overall_score`, `_time`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Land GRC or continuous-control scores into `compliance` or `grc` with stable `framework` labels and numeric `overall_score` (0–100); (2) align calendar quarters to your audit cycle (`span=90d` vs fiscal); (3) schedule weekly and snapshot results to a summary index for year-over-year evidence; (4) validate `predict` against low-volume series—disable or widen `period` if confidence bands explode; (5) pair the portfolio panel with the by-framework panel for board-ready trending.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance OR index=grc sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture") earliest=-730d
| eval fw=case(like(framework,"%NIST%"),"NIST_CSF",like(framework,"%ISO%"),"ISO27001",like(framework,"%SOC%"),"SOC2",1=1,framework)
| timechart span=3mon avg(overall_score) as avg_score by fw
| trendline sma2(NIST_CSF) as roll_NIST_CSF sma2(ISO27001) as roll_ISO27001 sma2(SOC2) as roll_SOC2
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compliance Posture Score Trending** — Rolling quarterly posture scores across NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and SOC 2 show whether investments in controls are improving measurable outcomes—not just checkbox activity—so executives and regulators see trajectory, not a one-time snapshot.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` OR `index=grc` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture")` — fields `framework`, `overall_score`, `_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), custom HTTP poller, or indexed CSV from Archer/ServiceNow GRC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance, grc.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, index=grc, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fw** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=3mon** buckets with a separate series **by fw** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Output fields: _time, avg_score, fw, portfolio_score.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line or area chart (portfolio score, `sma_posture`, `posture_forecast`), multiseries line (scores by `fw`), overlay confidence bands from `predict`.
grandmaExplanation: We monitor rolling quarterly posture scores across nist csf, iso 27001, and soc 2 show whether investments in controls are improving measurable outcomes—not just checkbox activity—so executives and regulators. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
